catalysts_general: Final federal construction license issued March 5, 2026; FID made; construction begins summer 2026 (4yr schedule); Rook I 30M lbs/yr production; analyst target $19.53 (+54%) (Tier 1: regulatory press release)

Discovery: Canadian Nuclear Safety Commission officially issued the site preparation and construction license for NexGen's Rook I project in March 2026 — the highest-grade undeveloped uranium deposit in the Athabasca Basin; Western utilities are paying jurisdictional premiums to secure non-Russian, non-Kazakh supply, and Rook I is the largest single new source of supply in a Western-aligned jurisdiction entering permitting.
value_picks.analyst_take: Final federal construction license issued March 5 and FID made — highest-grade undeveloped uranium deposit globally; 30M lb/yr = ~20% global supply from one mine

Bonita and OrbiMed Advisors LLC (a specialist biotech fund and company director) both purchased 2,815,315 shares each at $4.44 for $12.5M apiece — a simultaneous $25M combined commitment by a top-tier healthcare investor who sits on the board, suggesting imminent clinical or business development catalyst awareness.
value_picks.analyst_take: OrbiMed Advisors + Director simultaneously bought $12.5M each at $4.44 offering — specialist biotech fund conviction at offering price signals near-term catalyst awareness

Discovery: 10% owner Jorey Chernett has been relentlessly accumulating Neuronetics with zero sales — purchasing 500K shares in late March, 385K more on March 30, and another 200K on April 22 (total 2.9M+ shares, $3.95M+ in recent months); Canaccord maintains Strong Buy at $3.00 target (136% upside from ~$1.58), with Q1 beat ($41.8M rev vs $40.8M est) and positive outlook from Greenbrook acquisition expansion.
value_picks.analyst_take: Relentless 10% owner accumulation (200K shares April 20-21, 2.9M+ shares total); Canaccord $3 target = 78% upside; 27.3% SI creates squeeze fuel

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: FDA extended LNTH-2501 PDUFA to June 29 (manufacturing, not safety/efficacy); PYLARIFY revenue fell 9.7% YoY Q4; 2026 guide $1.4-1.45B below 2025's $1.54B; Truist $98, Mizuho $95, Jefferies $110 (Tier 1: company PR + 8-K)

Discovery: Lantheus is down ~30% from its 52-week high ($111.29) to ~$78, with a PDUFA decision date of June 29, 2026 for LNTH-2501 (Gallium 68 edotreotide for somatostatin receptor-positive NETs imaging) — critically, the 3-month FDA extension is manufacturing-related ONLY, not safety or efficacy, which is a positive signal; Q1 2026 earnings on May 7 is an additional 2-week catalyst; analysts are raising targets (Truist $98, Mizuho $95, Jefferies $110) with 11 buys and zero sells; bear is that any further manufacturing delay or CRL would reset the stock to low $60s.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Three 2026 pipeline catalysts (LNTH-2501 June 29, PNT2003 June, MK-6240 August) but PYLARIFY declining and manufacturing-flagged PDUFA extension raises CRL probability

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner

Discovery: Boston Scientific sits 41% below its 52-week high ($109.50) at ~$64.87 after jumping 9% on April 22 Q1 earnings beat (EPS $0.80 vs $0.79, revenue $5.20B vs $5.17B, +11.6% YoY) — analysts maintain overwhelmingly bullish stance (10 Strong Buy, 22 Buy) anchored on the FARAPULSE pulsed field ablation platform driving 18.2% cardiovascular growth — however, FY2026 guidance was CUT meaningfully (sales growth to 7-8.5% from 10.5-11.5%) and the Watchman device had disappointing trial data in March; next major catalyst is Q2 earnings in late July (just beyond 90 days), so this is a 'quality in macro panic' setup where the turnaround timeline is longer than ideal.
growth_picks.analyst_take: FARAPULSE competitive issue is structural — J&J Varipulse native CARTO integration is a workflow advantage requiring years to overcome; next catalyst Q2 earnings July 22
